body.u: When I left school at 16 I felt as though I was now grown up and I could leave the secruity of school and venture into the world outside.

What I wanted was to look after children either in a home or at home. But this was slightly harder than I had thought it would be. I had the choice of either going to college and doing a course or going to work until I was eighteen and then trying for a job.
Of course I took what I thought was the easy way out and took a job in a coop supermarket until I was eighteen and this was where the fun and games started.

The career’s officer was the first person I saw. She gave me form to fill in and said I would hear from her. Finally a letter came asking if I would like an interview at a National Children’s Home as a NurseyNursery Nurse. But when the day came they asked me what training I had had which of course was none so I was turned down. This went on I had about 4 interviews but in all of them they asked the same thing “have you had any training”. In the end I gave up. No job to go to on the dole!! Because I had worked for over six months I was picking up twenty-five a week unemployment benefit but this wasn’t what I wanted.
I had thought, while I was at school that it was easy, getting a job. I soon found out it wasn’t. I still remember sitting in my lessons not doing anything ‘thinking why bother’ but I wished I had.
The teachers weren’t really all that helpful not strict enough.

After two or three jobs in shops I saw the job advertised “child help needed. No Qualifications Necessary. Good with children” I applied and got the I have not looked back since. Altogether I have looked after kids of 3 different family’s watched them grow up. It was what I wanted I had fulfilled my ambition. I got married at 33 and had 2 children of my own one boy and one girl.
I thought that would have been the end of my career but No, I still looked after family’s until I was 58 and it was then I decided to retire to enjoy my grandchildren.

Now thinking back, I was lucky really to succeed in the job I wanted. If I had taken the opportunity of going college I would have done much better. You don’t know how hard it is until you go out into the world. I think children should be taught more about jobs and working so they what to expect so they don’t end up like a lot of people unemployed!!
